summary: Pass A backend validation: object storage uploads, /api/files roundtrip, wishlist, reviews, low-stock alerts, and order placement with no-op notifications. All 18 pytest tests PASS on first run against the public preview URL. Object storage is live (kind:'storage'), uploaded image bytes round-trip identically through /api/files/{path}, customer/admin RBAC enforced, verified_buyer flag flips correctly after a real order, low-stock filter respects threshold and ENV default of 3, and stock decrements correctly on order placement (p06 16→14). Resend/Twilio keys are blank and notify_service helpers no-op cleanly — order POST and admin status PUT both return 200, no 500s in backend logs.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/files/{path} is fully public — no signed URL or auth check. Acceptable for product images, but storage_path is a UUID under siri-boutique/products/ so this is hard to enumerate. Consider an HMAC/expiry token if any private media is ever stored in the same bucket.

POST /api/admin/uploads relies solely on the client-provided Content-Type header for type validation (no magic-byte sniff). A malicious admin could upload non-image bytes labelled image/jpeg. Low risk because endpoint is admin-only, but worth a `imghdr`/`Pillow` probe.

Stock decrement uses {stock: {$gt: 0}} guard but does NOT prevent overselling when current stock < qty (e.g., qty=5 against stock=3 will go to -2 because $inc -5 still fires; the $gt:0 only blocks at zero, not against the requested qty). Recommend matching {stock: {$gte: qty}} or rejecting the order pre-insert when any item over-orders.
place_order computes order_id from datetime.now().timestamp() %80000 + 4-hex suffix → small but real collision chance under burst load. Consider full uuid or atomic counter.

notify_service whatsapp send_whatsapp() returns False if normalize_indian_phone fails — good. order_confirmation_whatsapp/email never raise even with partial address keys (uses .get with default '') — good. Helpers are correctly fail-soft.

Storage init currently runs on every is_enabled()=True call path through put_image/get_image when _storage_key is None — fine, but a single startup init (already wired) plus retry-on-403 is the pattern; acceptable.
ALLOWED_IMAGE_TYPES does not include image/heic/heif which iPhone admins may attempt; minor UX point.
test_credentials: Admin admin@siriboutique.in / SiriAdmin@2026 ✓ ; Demo demo@siriboutique.in / demo1234 ✓ — both work on preview URL.
seed_data_creation: Tests created 2 demo orders (p07 qty=1, p06 qty=2) under demo@siriboutique.in, two reviews on p10/p07 (verified_buyer true/false), and uploaded 2 small images (1 jpeg, 1 png) into object storage. Stock for p06 dropped 16→14, p07 dropped 3→2 (now flagged in low-stock default-threshold list).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pass A is fully working on the public URL. Object storage is initialised at startup (confirmed in supervisor backend.out.log: 'Object storage initialized'). EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is set; RESEND_*/TWILIO_* are blank by design — notifications correctly no-op without raising. Note that p07 stock is now 2 (was 3) due to the verified-buyer test order; if you re-run tests, the test orders will continue to deplete p06/p07 stock — not a bug, just be aware. The test file /app/backend/tests/test_pass_a.py can be re-run any time and is self-contained (uses dynamic before/after stock checks).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — Pass A features all pass on first run. Minor code-quality observations captured under critical_code_review_comments for the main agent's consideration (most notable: oversell guard on stock decrement, content-type sniffing for uploads).
